prerequisites: An Algolia account with an application (create one in the Algolia dashboard).
Your Algolia Application ID (the application_id config field), found in the Algolia dashboard.
An Algolia API key with permission to search and read your indices (the api_key config field).
A start_date value to bound the data the connector reads. Optionally an object_id and/or search_query to target specific records.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Algolia's Search REST API authenticates each request with two values sent as HTTP headers: X-Algolia-Application-Id (your Application ID) and X-Algolia-API-Key (an API key). The connector takes the API key in its api_key secret field and the Application ID in its application_id field. Use an API key whose ACL permissions allow searching and reading the indices you want to sync.

setupSteps.title: Locate your Application ID and API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Algolia dashboard and open the API Keys settings (Settings > API Keys). Copy your Application ID and an API key whose ACL permissions grant the search and read access the connector needs. The Application ID is also part of your API base URL in the form https://{APPLICATION_ID}.algolia.net.

setupSteps.title: Provide the credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter your Application ID in the application_id field and your API key in the api_key field (stored as a secret). These map to Algolia's X-Algolia-Application-Id and X-Algolia-API-Key request headers; all requests are sent over HTTPS.

setupSteps.title: Configure the sync
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date to bound the records the connector reads. Optionally set object_id to target a specific record and/or search_query to scope which records are retrieved. Leave the optional fields empty to use the connector defaults.

sources.title: Algolia Search REST API reference (authentication headers)

Manage your Algolia applications (Application ID and API keys in the dashboard)
